summary:
Chief cells are located at the base of gastric glands specifically within the fundus of the stomach. This position places them in the oxyntic region alongside parietal cells, where they secrete pepsinogen essential for protein digestion. The fundamental distinction from other regions lies in their exclusive presence near the basal portion rather than more distal sections such as the neck or isthmus.
